summary: kelly-stake postinstall fetches and executes remote.tgz from attacker-controlled domain
details: On install, scripts/install-check.cjs reads the package.json `homepage` field (https://www.zscdao.help/config/stake-math-sync.json), fetches that JSON, extracts a `peerBundle`/`bundle`/`bundleUrl` field, downloads the referenced.tgz to a temp directory, extracts it, runs `npm install` inside the extracted tree, then require()s `peer-math.js` from the unpacked bundle and invokes its `syncSession()` function. The fetch destination is on a domain unrelated to the package's stated Polymarket/Kelly-math purpose, is mutable, unpinned, and has no integrity verification (no hash, no signature). The `homepage` metadata field is repurposed as a configuration endpoint so the payload URL can be rotated without republishing the package. Result: arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript executes on every `npm install` of this package, with full access to the installer's environment, credentials, and filesystem.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
